summary: Patrick McKenzie shares personal facts about himself as part of a blog meme chain in the micro-ISV community. He reveals his intense dislike of Tokyo despite living in Japan, his extensive job history of 16+ positions, his functional illiteracy in written Japanese despite working as a translator, his World of Warcraft guild leadership that preceded founding Bingo Card Creator, and his freakish ability to memorize entire books as a child. The post humanizes him beyond his professional persona and provides early glimpses of the personality traits that would define his later career.
key_points: McKenzie's departure from World of Warcraft directly led to founding Bingo Card Creator — the sudden surplus of free time and need for intellectual challenge drove him to build software
Despite working professionally as a translator/interpreter in Japan, he considers his Japanese literacy his biggest professional failing, able to handle technical jargon but not everyday instructions
He had held at least 16 different jobs by his mid-twenties, with almost all ending on good terms, suggesting a pattern of restless experimentation that would define his career
His early attempt at newspaper columnism ended partly due to death threats, foreshadowing his later preference for independent publishing on his own terms
money_quotes: I would rather live in Baghdad than Tokyo.
People who think videogames are mindless entertainment which teach no valuable skills have never attempted to create an equitable DKP system.
I could translate this post into Japanese without difficulty, given sufficient time and access to a computerized dictionary. I could not do it by hand without sounding like a slow third grader.
One of my major instigations for doing so was I suddenly had so much free time and nothing I could really sink my teeth into in terms of intellectual problems.
Professional relationships are about more than just accumulating Google juice and career advancement… not that those are bad things!
The pay is lousy, the deadlines are irksome, editors are a bear to deal with, and the death threats I got caused needless distress for my family, so I found greener pastures.
key_insight: The restless intellectual energy that drove McKenzie through 16 jobs, World of Warcraft guild leadership, and memorizing entire books is the same energy that would eventually make him a successful software entrepreneur — he just needed to find the right outlet.
